weather.tip: Overcast start at 21.4°C with a 94% chance of rain through the day — carry an umbrella on the commute and leave a buffer for waterlogging-prone stretches.

around.headline: Bengaluru forest officer loses nearly Rs 10 lakh after clicking on an APK file
around.summary: A forest department officer lost close to Rs 10 lakh after installing an app sent as an APK file, a common trick used to plant malware that reads OTPs and drains bank accounts. Never install apps from links in SMS/WhatsApp/email — stick to the Play Store or App Store.

around.headline: Bengaluru woman dies by suicide after being taunted over childlessness
around.summary: A woman died by suicide after facing repeated taunts over not having children, police said. If you or someone you know is under this kind of pressure, Karnataka's women's helpline (181) and local police both take these complaints seriously.

around.headline: Towing of abandoned vehicles begins in Bengaluru after seven-day notice period
around.summary: GBA and BTP have started towing vehicles left abandoned on city streets after a week-long notice window lapsed. If you've got a car or bike parked long-term on a public road, move it or risk it being towed.
around.source: The Hindu / The Times of India

around.headline: Karnataka High Court limits compensation in a borrowed-bike crash case
around.summary: The High Court narrowed the compensation payable in a case involving a crash on a borrowed two-wheeler, with implications for who's liable when you lend or borrow a vehicle. Check your insurance covers named/any-rider use before handing over your keys.

around.headline: Karnataka High Court quashes a 10-acre land acquisition, citing fraud and tampered records
around.summary: The court set aside the acquisition after finding the underlying documents had been fraudulently altered, the latest in a string of Bengaluru-area land fraud cases this month. Anyone buying land or a plot should independently verify title records rather than trust paperwork shown by a seller or broker.

around.headline: India asks citizens to postpone Iran travel as US-Iran tensions escalate
around.summary: With fresh US strikes and Strait of Hormuz shipping disruptions, the government has advised against travel to Iran and urged Indians already there to leave. If you have travel plans anywhere in the region, check the MEA advisory before booking.

events.name: Art Bengaluru Collective — 11th annual art exhibition
events.note: A citywide showcase of contemporary art now in its 11th edition — a good pick if you want a low-key gallery outing this week.

events.name: Tarangini 2026, headlined by Ustad Amjad Ali Khan
events.note: The renowned sarod maestro headlines this Bengaluru concert — worth booking ahead if you're into classical music.

events.name: CJP protest in Bengaluru
events.note: A citizens' protest is urging wider participation and sustained public questioning on civic issues — worth knowing about if you're near the venue or want to get involved.

street_smart.title: A WhatsApp message arrives with a link to install an app — maybe a 'parcel tracking' app, a 'pension verification' app, or something that looks like it's from a government office. What do you do?
street_smart.based_on: Real case reported today: a Bengaluru forest department officer lost nearly Rs 10 lakh after clicking on and installing an APK file sent to his phone (The Times of India).
street_smart.situation: You get an unsolicited message with a link to an APK file — an Android app installer that doesn't come from the Play Store. The message looks official, references something plausible (a delayed delivery, a pending verification, a government scheme), and asks you to install the app to proceed.

choices.text: Ignore the link entirely and, if you need the service it claims to offer, go find the official app on the Play Store or the organisation's real website.
choices.feedback: Correct. Legitimate courier, banking, and government services publish on the Play Store or App Store — they don't ask you to sideload an APK from a text message. Going direct to the official source removes the risk entirely.

choices.text: Install it, since the message looks official and it's 'just' meant to track a package or verify a form.
choices.feedback: This is exactly how today's Rs 10 lakh loss happened. Sideloaded APKs commonly request accessibility or SMS permissions that let scammers read your OTPs and silently authorise bank transfers — the innocent-sounding purpose is the bait, not the actual function of the app.

choices.text: Install it, but deny every permission it asks for afterward.
choices.feedback: Many malicious APKs are built to still function, or to nag you until you grant the permission they actually need, and some exploit the install process itself. Denying permissions after installing doesn't undo the fact that unknown code is already running on your phone — better to never install it at all.

choices.text: Forward the link to a tech-savvy friend or family member and wait for them to check it before deciding.
choices.feedback: Getting a second opinion isn't harmful by itself, but it wastes time you don't need to spend — there's no legitimate reason to install an APK from a text message in the first place, so the safer move is to simply not install it rather than debate whether it's safe.

takeaways: Never install APK files from links in SMS, WhatsApp, or email — only install apps from the Play Store or App Store, where Google/Apple screen for this kind of malware.

If you've already installed a suspicious APK, uninstall it immediately, switch to airplane mode to cut its network access, then check your bank statements and call your bank's helpline to freeze cards or accounts if anything looks off.

Report the incident fast to the National Cybercrime helpline 1930 or cybercrime.gov.in — banks can sometimes freeze a fraudulent transfer before it clears, but only if you report within the first few hours.

quiz.q: About how much money did a Bengaluru forest officer lose today after clicking on and installing an APK file sent to his phone?
quiz.why: Malicious APKs often request accessibility permissions, which let an attacker's software read on-screen OTPs and authorise transfers without ever needing your typed password.

quiz.q: At least how many people are feared dead in flash floods that hit Jammu & Kashmir, according to reports today?
quiz.why: Flash floods are common in Himalayan states during the July–August monsoon peak, when saturated hillsides and swollen rivers can turn dangerous with little warning.

quiz.q: Karnataka High Court quashed a 10-acre land acquisition today after finding what problem with the underlying paperwork?
quiz.why: It's the latest in a run of Bengaluru-area land fraud cases this month — always verify title records independently rather than trusting documents shown by a seller or broker.

quiz.q: What is the minimum age required to contest for a seat in India's Lok Sabha?
quiz.why: The minimum age is lower for the Lok Sabha than the Rajya Sabha, where a candidate must be at least 30.

quiz.q: Which gas do plants primarily absorb from the atmosphere during photosynthesis?
quiz.why: Plants take in carbon dioxide and release oxygen as a byproduct — the reverse of what animals do when they respire.
